explanation:
**Nutrition and fitness** rest on physical principles (energy balance) and biological factors (macronutrients, micronutrients). This calculator uses formulas validated by the scientific community (ACSM, ISSN, NIH).

## How It's Calculated

How to distribute calories across your 8-hour eating window.

This calculator applies the formula to your input values, checking typical ranges and alerting you to any values that fall outside reasonable parameters.

## Standard Caloric Distribution

| Macronutrient | % of Calories | Calories per Gram |
|---|---|---|
| Protein | 15-35% | 4 kcal/g |
| Carbohydrates | 45-65% | 4 kcal/g |
| Fats | 20-35% | 9 kcal/g |
| Alcohol | — | 7 kcal/g |
| Fiber | Minimum 25-38g/day | — |

## When to Consult a Registered Dietitian

- If you have conditions (diabetes, hypertension, kidney disease) requiring personalized nutrition.
- If you want to gain significant muscle mass or compete in sports.
- If you have disordered eating patterns (binge eating, extreme restriction).
- If you haven't made progress toward your goal for more than 3 months.
- To plan complete vegan or vegetarian nutrition.

## Important Notes

This calculation is for reference only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a qualified professional. Values are current for 2026 and are reviewed regularly.

faq.a: 16/8 intermittent fasting is an eating pattern where you fast for 16 hours and eat during an 8-hour window each day. It's a simple approach to time-restricted eating.

faq.q: How many calories should I eat per day?
faq.a: This depends on age, sex, weight, and activity level. On average: adult women need 1,800–2,200 calories; adult men need 2,200–3,000 calories. Use a TDEE calculator for your exact needs.

faq.q: What should I eat during my eating window?
faq.a: Focus on whole foods: lean proteins, vegetables, fruits, whole grains, and healthy fats. Avoid ultra-processed foods. Quality matters as much as quantity for long-term success.

faq.q: Can I lose weight fast with intermittent fasting?
faq.a: Healthy weight loss is 0.5–1 kg per week. Faster loss often leads to muscle loss and rebound weight gain. Slow, consistent progress is sustainable and preserves lean mass.

faq.q: How much protein do I need daily?
faq.a: Sedentary: 0.8g per kg of body weight. Active: 1.2–1.5g per kg. Building muscle: 1.6–2.2g per kg of lean mass. More than 2.2g/kg provides diminishing returns.
For most healthy adults, 16/8 fasting is safe. However, consult your doctor if you have medical conditions, take medications, are pregnant/breastfeeding, or have a history of eating disorders.
Creatine and whey protein have strong evidence. BCAAs and multivitamins have weaker support. Remember: a solid diet beats supplements. Whole foods should be your foundation.

faq.q: Can I exercise while doing 16/8 fasting?
faq.a: Yes, but timing matters. Train during or near your eating window for fuel and recovery. Experiment to find what feels best for your energy and performance.

faq.q: How long until I see results?
faq.a: Changes in energy and focus often appear within 1–2 weeks. Body composition changes typically show within 4–6 weeks with consistent diet and exercise.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Formula is fixed; no external data updates required
